_help.allImgCodebookFile: Whether to use all images for codebook construction. If codebook file is true then this parameter determines if the code looks for a codebook made with the entire dataset or one built on a subset of images.
_help.blockOccupancy: How much the mask [if provided] should fill the block being analyzed. A value of 0.5 means that at least 50% of the block is covered by the mask.
_help.blockOverlap: The overlap between adjacent blocks in the HOG feature extraction. A value of 0.5 means that blocks will overlap by 50%.
_help.blockSize: The number of cells in each block for HOG feature extraction. A block is a larger region that contains multiple cells, and the HOG features are normalized within each block. A common choice is 2, which means each block will contain 2x2 cells.
_help.cGrid: The grid of C values for hyperparameter tuning.
_help.calcAttnToMask: Do you want to calculate the overlap of attention to masks?
_help.cellSize: The size of each cell in the HOG feature extraction. A common choice is 2, which means each cell will be 2x2x2 voxels.
_help.classNames: List of class names for analysis. For example ['Control', 'LOF'] or ['CGN_Control', 'GNP_nipblLOF']. These names are used for labeling the classes in the analysis and should correspond to the conditions/groups in your dataset. Separate conditions by _ or spaces or -.
_help.classWeight: The class weight for the logistic regression model.
_help.codebookFile: Whether to look for a pre-existing codebook. if false, it will generate a new codebook. If true, it will look for a codebook with the name specified by the parameters above. If it can't find a codebook with that name, it will generate a new codebook.
_help.dictionarySize: The size of the visual words codebook.
_help.hogNormalization: How do you want to normalize the HOG descriptors? Options are 'asinh', '5-95', or 'none'. The 5-95 method scales the descriptor values based on the 5th and 95th percentiles, which can help mitigate the influence of outliers and improve the robustness of the descriptors.
_help.l1Grid: The grid of l1 values for hyperparameter tuning.
_help.lassoAlpha: The lasso alpha parameter for codebook construction.
_help.lrC: The regularization strength for the logistic regression model.
_help.lrPenalty: The penalty term for the logistic regression model.
_help.nJobs: How many parallel jobs do you want to run when processing HOG features? If nJobs is too high you might run out of memory and crash the program.
_help.nSplits: The number of folds for cross-validation.
_help.normSmooth: Whether to smooth the normalized features for Tfidf normalization.
_help.normSubLinear: Whether to use sub-linear normalization  for Tfidf normalization.
_help.normalizeMethod: The normalization method for the feature vectors. Options are: tfidf, l2 norm, none, L2 tfidf, l2 exclude tfidf, tfidf L2, tfidf L2 threshold, l1 norm, tfidf l1
_help.numberOfNeighbors: The number of neighbors to consider for k-NN based methods.
_help.phiHistogramBins: The number of bins for the phi histogram in the HOG feature extraction. A common choice is 36, which means the phi histogram will have 36 bins.
_help.pooling: The pooling method for sparse word assignment for patch analysis. Options are: sum or max.
_help.removeSmallBadCrops: Do you want to remove small crops that are smaller than a certain threshold?
_help.runLRSweep: Whether to run a hyperparameter sweep for the logistic regression model, managed through Weights and Biases.
_help.runStreamlitApp: Do you want to run the streamlit app automatically after the analysis is complete?
_help.siftEdgeR: Default 6, increase to find more 'edge like' keypoints
_help.siftKeypointsLocation: Where are the keypoints localized to? Areas of high signal, low signal or both [all]?
_help.siftNormalization: How do you want to normalize the SIFT descriptors? Options are 'asinh', '5-95', or 'none'. The 5-95 method scales the descriptor values based on the 5th and 95th percentiles, which can help mitigate the influence of outliers and improve the robustness of the descriptors.
_help.siftThreshold: Default 0.03, decrease to find more keypoints, 0.09 worked well for subset of Chris' 3D data
_help.singleChannel: Do you want to process only a single channel?
_help.sparsityAlpha: The sparsity alpha parameter for codebook construction.
_help.splitSmallAndLarge: Do you want to split the images into small and large categories for processing?
_help.thetaHistogramBins: The number of bins for the theta histogram in the HOG feature extraction. A common choice is 18, which means the theta histogram will have 18 bins.
_help.transferFiles: Do you want to transfer the processed files to a different location? This is useful if you are running the pipeline on a server and want to save the results locally/different location.
_help.useSegmentationMasks: Do you want to use segmentation masks for image processing?
_help.zDriveSaveFolder: If you want to transfer the processed files to a different location, specify the path to the folder where you want to save the files. Make sure to include the trailing slash. This is helpful if you need to run the code on a server and want to save the results locally or in a different location.
_help.zModifier: A modifier to help with the anisotropicity found in many microscopy volumes. This is used to scale the z-axis of the HOG feature extraction. A value of 0.5 means that the z-axis will be scaled by 0.5.
